concepts.title: Basic Concepts
concepts.subtitle: Essential eSIM information

whatIsESIM.question: What is eSIM? Why do I need it?
whatIsESIM.answer: An eSIM (embedded SIM) is a digital SIM card built into your device. Unlike traditional physical SIM cards, eSIMs can be activated instantly without inserting a physical card. You need it for convenient international travel, switching carriers instantly, and having multiple phone numbers on one device. It eliminates the need to find local SIM cards when traveling and provides instant connectivity in over 190 countries.

importantInfo.question: Important Information Before Using eSIM
importantInfo.answer: Before using eSIM, ensure: 1) Your device is eSIM compatible and carrier unlocked, 2) You have a stable internet connection for activation, 3) Your device isn't already using maximum eSIM profiles, 4) You understand that some eSIM plans are one-time use only, 5) Keep your QR code safe as it can't be regenerated in most cases, 6) Backup your current SIM settings before switching.

phoneSupport.question: How to check if my phone supports eSIM
phoneSupport.answer: To check eSIM compatibility: iPhone (XS/XR and newer): Settings > Cellular > Add Cellular Plan. Android: Settings > Network & Internet > Mobile Network > Advanced > Carrier. Look for 'Add eSIM' or 'Download a SIM instead' options. You can also dial *#06# to see if an EID number appears. Popular compatible devices include iPhone XS+, Google Pixel 3+, Samsung Galaxy S20+, and most recent flagships.

findQR.question: Where I can find QR
findQR.answer: Your eSIM QR code is sent via email after purchase completion. Check your email inbox and spam folder for the confirmation message. The QR code is also available in your account dashboard under 'My eSIMs' section. You can also access it through our mobile app in the 'Active Plans' or 'My Orders' section. Keep this QR code safe as it's usually single-use only.

checkBalance.question: How to check my balance
checkBalance.answer: Check your balance through: 1) Our mobile app - Account tab shows current balance, 2) Your device's cellular settings showing remaining data, 3) SMS notifications we send for usage updates, 4) Our website dashboard after logging in, 5) Contact support for detailed usage breakdown. Most devices also show data usage in Settings > Cellular/Mobile Data > Current Period.

iccidNumber.question: Where I can find the ICCID number for eSIM
iccidNumber.answer: Find your eSIM ICCID: iPhone: Settings > General > About > scroll to find ICCID. Android: Settings > About Phone > Status > ICCID. You can also dial *#06# to display all identifiers including ICCID. The ICCID is also printed on your original eSIM QR code email and available in your account dashboard. This 19-20 digit number uniquely identifies your eSIM.

deleteESIM.question: How to delete eSIM
deleteESIM.answer: To remove eSIM: iPhone: Settings > Cellular > select eSIM line > Remove Cellular Plan > confirm removal. Android: Settings > Network & Internet > Mobile Network > Advanced > select eSIM > Delete/Remove. Warning: Deleting an eSIM permanently removes it from your device. You cannot reinstall the same eSIM profile again - you'll need to purchase a new one.

concepts.tip1: Always ensure stable internet before eSIM activation
concepts.tip2: Keep your QR code safe - it's usually single-use only
concepts.tip4: Contact support if you encounter any issues
